name: Jinmenju
description: The Jinmenju (人面樹, 'human-faced tree') is a strange and unsettling tree from Japanese folklore. It is said to grow in remote mountain valleys or deep within forests. Its most distinctive feature is the fruit it bears: human heads. These heads are not merely shaped like human faces, but are fully formed, lifelike human heads, complete with expressions. Unlike normal fruit, these heads do not drop silently. Instead, when they ripen and fall from the branches, they laugh uproariously before plummeting to the ground. This unsettling laughter is both eerie and disturbing, adding to the Jinmenju's reputation as a creature of nightmare. The tree itself is described as large and ancient, with thick, gnarled branches capable of supporting the weight of its unusual fruit. The number of heads on a single tree can vary, but accounts often speak of dozens or even hundreds of these laughing faces hanging from its limbs. The presence of a Jinmenju is considered a bad omen, a sign of misfortune or impending doom. Some legends suggest the heads are the spirits of the dead, trapped within the fruit as punishment or warning. Others believe the tree is a demonic entity, feeding off the fear and despair of those who witness its macabre display. The Jinmenju serves as a potent symbol of the uncanny and the grotesque, highlighting the darker aspects of Japanese folklore and the fear of the unknown that permeates the natural world.
subtitle: The Human-Faced Laughing Tree
appearance: A large, ancient tree with thick, gnarled branches. Its fruit are fully formed human heads, complete with lifelike expressions. These heads laugh uproariously before falling from the branches.
habitat: Remote mountain valleys and deep forests in Japan.
behavior: The Jinmenju passively grows its human-faced fruit. When ripe, the heads laugh loudly before falling. Its presence is a harbinger of bad luck, causing fear and unease in those who encounter it. It is generally considered a stationary, passive entity.

keyMyths.title: The Laughing Harvest
keyMyths.description: A traveler stumbles upon a Jinmenju and is driven mad by the constant, unsettling laughter of the falling heads. He is forever haunted by the image of the faces.

sources.author: Sekien Toriyama
sources.description: A collection of yokai illustrations, including a depiction of the Jinmenju.
sources.text: A collection of yokai illustrations, including a depiction of the Jinmenju.

sources.author: Shigeru Mizuki
sources.description: Although not a direct source, Mizuki's work popularized many yokai, including the Jinmenju, in modern culture.
